acceptance: delete_link handler (when implemented) removes both __axon_links__ and __axon_links_rev__ entries; inject_isolation_violation deletes both forward and reverse-index entries; no orphaned reverse-index entries remain after link deletion in any test
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
Spec: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:437, crates/axon-sim/src/cycle.rs:270
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: create_link writes reverse-index entries to __axon_links_rev__ but no code path removes them when links are deleted. The inject_isolation_violation test helper deletes forward links from __axon_links__ without cleaning up the reverse-index, leaving orphaned entries that would cause delete_entity to falsely reject deletions. When a delete_link handler is implemented, reverse-index cleanup must be included.
Suggested fix: (1) When building the delete_link handler, delete the reverse-index entry alongside the forward link using Link::rev_storage_id(). (2) Update inject_isolation_violation in cycle.rs to also delete the __axon_links_rev__ entry to keep test state consistent.
title: correctness: reverse-index entries orphaned on link deletion

acceptance: Two concurrent /mcp/sse clients under the same authenticated actor but without an explicit session token do not share subscriptions; only the client that performed resources/subscribe receives the subsequent resource_updated notification.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-server/src/mcp_http.rs:282
Category: security
Severity: high
Description: session_id_from_request() falls back to identity.actor whenever the caller omits ?session= and x-axon-mcp-session. Because the HTTP router keys durable MCP state by (database, session_id), every client authenticated as the same actor is silently merged into one shared MCP session. In --no-auth mode that means all remote clients share the single anonymous session; with Tailscale auth, multiple MCP clients on the same node share subscriptions and unsubscriptions. I reproduced this against a live server on 2026-04-09 by opening two independent GET /mcp/sse connections without a session id, issuing exactly one resources/subscribe call over POST /mcp, and then updating tasks/t-001: both SSE clients received resource_updated even though only one client subscribed. This violates FEAT-016 US-055 acceptance that multiple subscriptions work independently and creates cross-client notification leakage/interference.
Suggested fix: Generate a transport-scoped session identifier when the client does not supply one, or require an explicit server-issued session token that both /mcp and /mcp/sse must present. Do not derive session identity from the authenticated actor alone.
notes:

acceptance: Qualified update and patch operations against same-named collections in different namespaces succeed without partial writes; index maintenance, gate-result persistence, audit entries, and API responses all use the qualified collection identity; regression tests cover indexed and/or gated namespaced updates and patches through the handler or HTTP surface.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:390
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:399
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:410
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:434
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:441
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:519
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:528
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:539
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:563
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:570
File: crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs:316
File: crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs:824
File: crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:568
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: compare_and_swap now canonicalizes the stored entity back to the bare collection name, and update_entity/patch_entity reuse that bare name for post-write side effects and the API response. In a FEAT-014 setup where prod.billing.invoices and prod.engineering.invoices both exist, indexed or gated updates patch the entity first and then call update_indexes, update_compound_indexes, put_gate_results, and audit logging with bare invoices. Those storage helpers resolve collection identity again and reject the ambiguous bare name, so the handler can return an error after the entity row has already been updated, leaving indexes, gate materialization, and audit inconsistent with persisted data. Even when no secondary maintenance runs, the update/patch response and audit entry lose the qualified collection identity.
Suggested fix: Preserve the qualified/requested collection identity across OCC update paths. Either have storage return the qualified collection it resolved, or have the handler keep using req.collection for every post-write side effect, audit entry, and response presentation. Add regression tests that update and patch indexed or gated entities in two same-named namespaces through the handler/API and prove the operation succeeds atomically with qualified collection identity preserved end to end.
title: correctness: namespaced update and patch reuse bare collection after OCC

acceptance: No route path conflicts: creating and retrieving an entity with ID query works correctly. Query endpoint is accessible at a non-conflicting path. HTTP integration tests cover both cases.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs:507
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: /entities/{collection}/query (POST) is registered alongside /entities/{collection}/{id} (GET/PUT/DELETE/POST). Axum matchit router prioritizes literal segments over captures. Any request to /entities/<col>/query regardless of method matches the literal route first. GET /entities/tasks/query (fetching entity with ID query) returns 405 instead of the entity. POST /entities/tasks/query (creating entity with ID query) routes to the query handler instead. This breaks API consistency for the reserved ID query and conflicts with POST /entities/{collection}/{id} for entity creation.
Suggested fix: Move query endpoint to a non-conflicting path such as POST /collections/{collection}/query or POST /query/{collection}.
title: integration: route path conflict — query literal shadows entity {id} capture

acceptance: Either: (1) audit entries are written within the StorageAdapter transaction before commit_tx(), verified by a test that crashes between commit and audit-append and shows no gap; OR (2) ADR-003 and ADR-004 explicitly document the post-commit audit strategy with a documented recovery mechanism, and INV-003 audit completeness invariant accounts for the gap
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-004-transaction-model.md:91-92
File: crates/axon-api/src/transaction.rs:144-151
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: ADR-004 (line 91-92) and ADR-003 (audit write path diagram) both specify that audit entries are written in the same storage transaction as entity mutations, ensuring atomic commit/rollback. However, transaction.rs writes audit entries AFTER commit_tx() succeeds (line 144-151). The code comment explicitly says 'The storage transaction is already durable at this point, so audit failures do not undo the committed data.' This creates a crash-safety gap: if the process dies between commit_tx() and audit append, committed mutations have no audit trail. This contradicts principle P2 (Audit is Not Optional - 100% mutation coverage).
Suggested fix: Either (a) refactor the AuditLog to write through the StorageAdapter transaction handle so audit entries are part of the same storage transaction, or (b) update ADR-003 and ADR-004 to document the post-commit audit strategy and add a recovery mechanism (e.g., write-ahead intent log or audit reconciliation on startup).
title: correctness: audit entries written post-commit contradicts ADR-003/ADR-004 same-transaction guarantee

acceptance: A test exists that calls create_collection with an invalid entity_schema, asserts the call fails, and then asserts the collection does NOT appear in list_collections (i.e. no orphan registration).
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:525-526
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: create_collection calls register_collection (line 525) then put_schema (line 526) sequentially. If put_schema fails (e.g. invalid entity_schema that fails compile_entity_schema), the collection is left registered but schemaless. This violates the documented invariant 'schemaless collections are not supported' (FEAT-001). A subsequent create_collection call for the same name would fail with AlreadyExists. The user would need to drop_collection and retry. The same atomicity gap exists with the audit append on line 528 — a schema+collection could be persisted with no audit trail.
Suggested fix: Either (a) validate the schema before calling register_collection (move put_schema or at least compile_entity_schema before the register call), or (b) roll back register_collection on put_schema failure. Option (a) is simpler and sufficient since validation is the likely failure mode.
notes: BLOCKED [2026-04-05T18:03:27-04:00]: intractable after 4 attempts with exponential backoff
title: correctness: create_collection is not atomic — put_schema failure leaves orphan collection

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml:49
Category: drift (rust-cargo)
Severity: medium
Description: The reviewed commit adds reqwest directly under crates/axon-server dev-dependencies instead of threading the new third-party crate through [workspace.dependencies] and consuming it with reqwest.workspace = true. The active rust-cargo concern explicitly requires new dependencies to be added to the workspace manifest first so versioning, audits, and future upgrades stay centralized. This patch followed that rule for uuid but not for reqwest, so the dependency addition is inconsistent with the repository's declared Rust practices.
Suggested fix: Add reqwest to [workspace.dependencies] in the workspace Cargo.toml, switch crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml to reqwest.workspace = true while keeping the needed stream feature in the workspace declaration, and re-run the dependency hygiene gates.

acceptance: Memory, SQLite, and Postgres adapters can register `invoices` in both `prod.billing` and `prod.engineering` without collision; listing each namespace returns only its own collection; dropping one namespace or database does not remove or corrupt the same-named collection in another namespace; automated regression tests cover all three adapters
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-core/src/id.rs:74-90
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:1153-1159
File: crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs:497-501
File: crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs:80-87
File: crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs:827-833
File: crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:67-75
File: crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:685-689
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: FEAT-014 requires collection names to be unique within a schema so `prod.billing.invoices` and `prod.engineering.invoices` can coexist. The new catalog persistence still keys collection identity by the bare `CollectionId` string everywhere. `CollectionId` has no namespace component, `create_collection` still rejects any name already present in the global `list_collections()` set and always registers into `default.default`, MemoryStorageAdapter maps `collection_namespaces` by bare collection ID, and the durable adapters keep `collections.name` as the primary key and `collection_views` keyed only by that name. As a result, the second same-named collection across namespaces is either rejected or silently collapsed onto the first one, so namespace isolation and force-drop semantics cannot be correct once real namespace-scoped collection creation lands.
Suggested fix: Thread namespace-qualified collection identity through the core model and storage adapters. At minimum, make collection catalog keys unique on `(database_name, schema_name, name)`, update `CollectionId` or add a distinct fully-qualified identifier for namespace-aware paths, and add regression tests proving the same collection name can exist independently in multiple namespaces across Memory, SQLite, and Postgres.
title: correctness: namespace catalogs still collapse collections by bare name

acceptance: Calling fetchCollections() twice against a backend without the helper contract performs exactly one __schema probe, both calls reject with the helper-contract error, and the transient-failure retry test still passes
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: ui/src/lib/graphql.ts:109
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new catch path clears helperContractCheck for every rejection, including the intentional GRAPHQL_HELPER_CONTRACT_ERROR raised when the backend schema truly lacks the helper contract. That changes the client from caching a permanent unsupported-schema failure to re-running the introspection probe on every helper call. Reproduced with bun against ui/src/lib/graphql.ts by calling fetchCollections() twice against an unsupported schema; the current code issues 2 __schema requests instead of failing the second call from cache.
Suggested fix: Only clear helperContractCheck for retryable transport/query failures, or cache the unsupported-schema error separately so permanent contract mismatches still fail fast without another probe.
title: correctness: preserve cached unsupported GraphQL helper-contract failures

description:
<context-digest>
<scope>policy-intents-ui backend prerequisite</scope>
<principles>Specification Completeness · Test-First Development · P2 Audit is Not Optional · P4 Transactions Mean Transactions</principles>
<concerns>rust-cargo · security-owasp (all)</concerns>
<governing>FEAT-030-mutation-intents-approval.md (Specified, P0); ADR-019 §6 (mutation intents bind preview/approval/execution, intent token HMAC format, pre-image versioning, commit validation); implementation-plan.md:84 FEAT-030 "Not started — axon-api, axon-graphql, axon-mcp, axon-audit"; §3a Priority #2 "FEAT-030 mutation preview, approval, and intent execution"; no existing tracker bead governs this work.</governing>
<adrs>ADR-019 · ADR-012 · ADR-013 · ADR-018</adrs>
<align>hx-935600fc · AR-2026-04-22-policy-intents-ui.md §10 hx-5e34a26c-slot</align>
</context-digest>
Stand up the FEAT-030 backend execution plan as a parent bead with decomposed children. Work slices should include:
1. Intent record system collection scoped per tenant/database (axon-api + axon-storage).
2. Canonical operation hash and pre-image version capture across single- and multi-entity operations.
3. `previewMutation` / `commitMutationIntent` / `approveMutationIntent` / `rejectMutationIntent` GraphQL fields and `pendingMutationIntents` / `mutationIntent(id)` queries (axon-graphql).
4. MCP mirror outputs: `allowed`, `needs_approval`, `denied`, `conflict` with intent token (axon-mcp).
5. Intent token: opaque server-side reference with HMAC; commit validation re-checks every binding dimension.
6. Audit integration: approvals, rejections, expirations, commits all audited with intent ID, policy decision trace, approver identity.
7. Test coverage: SCN-017 approval path, stale-intent rejection (entity/policy/schema/grant/operation-hash), multi-entity intent binding, replay of approved + committed intents, MCP structured outcomes.
Depends on FEAT-029 backend (hx-44bdafd1 or its successor) because intent envelopes reuse the `PolicyPlan` decision engine.

acceptance: `role_from_tags()` returns `Role::Admin` for mixed-order tag sets containing `tag:axon-admin`, returns `Role::Write` for mixed-order sets containing write/agent tags but no admin tag, preserves the configured default for unrecognized tags, and regression tests cover at least read+admin and read+write permutations.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs:271
File: crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs:428
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `role_from_tags()` returns on the first recognized tag instead of computing the highest privilege across the full tag set. With a valid Tailscale response such as `["tag:axon-read", "tag:axon-admin"]`, the function returns `Role::Read` and silently downgrades an admin node based purely on tag ordering. FEAT-012 explicitly requires that when a node has multiple role-granting tags, the highest-privilege role wins (`admin > write > read`). The new tests only cover single-tag inputs, so this regression path is untested.
Suggested fix: Evaluate the complete tag list and return the maximum privilege present, then add regression tests for mixed-order multi-tag cases such as read+admin and read+write.
title: correctness: Tailscale role mapping depends on tag order

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
Spec: docs/helix/03-test/test-plan.md (PROP-002)
File: crates/axon-api/src/proptest_api.rs:31-96
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The test plan specifies PROP-002 as covering any sequence of CRUD operations. The implemented test only generates create+update sequences. Delete operations are never exercised, so audit reconstruction for entity deletion is unverified. The test also checks only the last audit entry rather than replaying the full log.
Suggested fix: Extend the proptest strategy to include delete operations and add a replay loop that walks the full audit log to reconstruct final state.
title: correctness: PROP-002 does not test audit reconstruction for delete operations

acceptance: `scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py .ddx/beads.jsonl --id hx-270f2f76` exits 0, and `ddx bead show hx-270f2f76 --json` reports a `<measure-results><timestamp>` that is no later than the record's `updated_at` while preserving the durable close-summary evidence for hx-6064816c.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:206
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The record appended by commit 7507c15ddcfe5642317c8dc278c1bdc0d66f20bf for review-finding hx-270f2f76 writes `<measure-results><timestamp>2026-04-09T18:53:00Z</timestamp>`, but that same JSONL row has `updated_at=2026-04-09T18:52:43.230223895Z`. The repository's own validator reproduces the inconsistency with `scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py .ddx/beads.jsonl --id hx-270f2f76`, which exits 1 and reports the measure timestamp is later than `updated_at`. HELIX review evidence is meant to be durable and internally consistent; future-dated measurement notes on the governing bead weaken tracker auditability and regress the timestamp invariant that was already important enough to add a dedicated checker.
Suggested fix: Rewrite the hx-270f2f76 record so its `<measure-results><timestamp>` is derived from the actual write time and does not exceed that row's `updated_at`, then re-run the scoped timestamp validator for hx-270f2f76. If the tracker write path can still generate this skew automatically, harden it or extend the regression coverage accordingly.
Close summary: Rewrote the closed hx-270f2f76 tracker record so its single <measure-results><timestamp> is derived from the actual update write time, remains <= updated_at, and preserves the durable closure evidence for hx-6064816c.
Verification: scripts/check_tracker_measure_timestamps.py .ddx/beads.jsonl --id hx-270f2f76; ddx bead show hx-270f2f76 --json; cargo check; cargo test; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo fmt --check.

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:668
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 424f98dec382e45bca59352bdd2ae4690297c3ec is the first commit that appends a closed hx-f5909336 row to .ddx/beads.jsonl, but that new row records both `closing_commit_sha` and the top-level `<report-summary><commit>` as c61d25057883da2d7c283abce7b3500bd2680ca6. As a result, tracker queries against the latest closed hx-f5909336 record misattribute which commit actually measured and closed the governing bead, breaking the durable traceability required by HELIX report semantics. Evidence: `git show HEAD~1:.ddx/beads.jsonl | rg ""id":"hx-f5909336""` shows only the in-progress row, while the closed row first appears in `git show HEAD:.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
Suggested fix: Append a new closed hx-f5909336 row that preserves the existing PARTIAL measure-results block but updates both `closing_commit_sha` and the top-level `<report-summary><commit>` to the commit that actually appends the corrective closure row, with the summary text naming that same commit.

acceptance: With `AXON_TEST_POSTGRES` unset and `DOCKER_HOST=tcp://127.0.0.1:1`, `cargo test -p axon-storage` completes without failing in PostgreSQL test setup; an explicit Postgres verification path remains available and documented.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:540
Category: drift (rust-cargo)
Severity: medium
Description: The two PostgreSQL-backed tests are now unconditional members of the default `cargo test -p axon-storage` suite. When `AXON_TEST_POSTGRES` is unset and a container runtime is unavailable, the tests panic during setup instead of skipping or being moved behind an explicit integration gate. Reproduction: `env -u AXON_TEST_POSTGRES DOCKER_HOST=tcp://127.0.0.1:1 cargo test -p axon-storage postgres_roundtrip_when_available -- --nocapture` fails at `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs:641` with `failed to start PostgreSQL test container`. That regresses the previous behavior where the crate's default test suite remained runnable without external infrastructure, and it conflicts with the rust-cargo practice that infra-dependent suites should be excluded from pure-unit CI/default test runs.
Suggested fix: Gate the Postgres-backed tests so the default `cargo test -p axon-storage` suite remains green without Docker or `AXON_TEST_POSTGRES` (for example by skipping with a clear message when neither is available, or by moving them behind an explicit integration feature/ignored test target) while preserving an explicit path that exercises the Postgres adapter in automated environments.
notes:
<measure-results>

    <criterion name="explicit Postgres verification path remains documented" status="pass" evidence="Updated crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs test module comment to point at AXON_TEST_POSTGRES=... cargo test -p axon-storage postgres::tests:: -- --nocapture"/>
  </acceptance>
  <gates>

    <gate concern="rust-cargo" command="cargo fmt --all -- --check" status="pass"/>
    <gate concern="rust-cargo" command="cargo deny check" status="pass"/>
    <gate concern="rust-cargo" command="cargo machete --with-metadata crates/axon-storage" status="pass"/>
  </gates>

That new hx-910e7983 record has no "notes" field at all, so it carries neither the <measure-results> block required by /home/erik/Projects/helix/workflows/references/measure.md nor the top-level <report-summary> required by /home/erik/Projects/helix/workflows/references/report.md. As a result,  fails with missing measurement and report evidence, and later tracker queries cannot recover what verified or closed the governing bead even though the row is marked closed.
Suggested fix: Append a new closed hx-910e7983 row that preserves the existing acceptance/description fields and adds durable notes containing a machine-readable <measure-results> block plus a top-level <report-summary> naming commit 438423c3dd73874064660c5817ae45ef5631dd9c and the verified artifact for the execution that closed hx-910e7983.

acceptance: axon-render exposes a reusable compiled-template API; the markdown GET path reuses compiled templates per collection instead of reparsing on every request; a test covers cache invalidation after template update
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-render/src/lib.rs:260
Category: performance
Severity: high
Description: `axon_render::render` calls `Template::new(template)` on every render. FEAT-026 requires compiled templates to be cached per collection and the documented handler flow says `axon-api` should call `axon_render::render`. With the current public API, downstream code cannot satisfy the per-collection cache requirement without bypassing the crate abstraction and reparsing the template on every request.
Suggested fix: Introduce a reusable compiled-template API in `axon-render` (for example a wrapper type around `ramhorns::Template` or a `compile()` + `render_compiled()` pair) and update the GET render path to cache compiled templates per collection, invalidating that cache on template updates.
title: performance: render() recompiles templates on every call
